rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RUIZ JR VS. PINOCCI CORP., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH RUIZ's Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION, KAROL MEIER and SANDI SELIG-FARNEY'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The motion is denied. Judgment was entered on April 16, 2014, and the Court lacks the authority to grant leave to amend because the judgment has not been vacated. See Young v. Berry Equipment Rentals, Inc. (1976) 55 Cal.App.3d 35, 38 ("Amendments proffered after judgment is rendered, however, are allowed only if the judgment is vacated as by granting a motion for new trial."); LaForge v. Groendyke (1952) 108 Cal.App.2d 522, 525 ("Ordinarily amendments after judgment are allowed only on an application for relief, and after the judgment has been vacated."). The Court denies defendant's request for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.5 because that statute does not apply to this action. Plaintiff filed this action on June 29, 2012, and CCP 128.5 only applies to actions that were commenced prior to December 31, 1994. See CCP 128.5(b)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FLYNN AND ENSLOW, INC. VS. DARREN WELTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Filed Concurrently With Defendants Demurrer As To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint And Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER'S MOTION To Strike. Denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A-C and H, and denied as to all other exhibits. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit A. The declarations and attached exhibits submitted by both parties were not considered in ruling on this motion to strike, as a motion to strike can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or matters that are judicially noticeable. (CCP sec. 437(a).) Although Plaintiff amended its complaint beyond the 10 days allowed in this Court's previous order, it serves judicial economy to allow the amendments to stand. Defendant has not shown that he is prejudiced by the untimely amendment. There is no trial date set. Therefore, if Plaintiff would have sought leave to amend after the 10 days expired, such relief would have been granted, given the strong policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. (See e.g., Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Defendant's motion to strike the references in the Second Amended Complaint to Defendant being a vice president or executive is also denied. An individual could hold the titles of operations manager, vice president, and executive at the same time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FLYNN AND ENSLOW, INC. VS. DARREN WELTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A-C and H, and denied as to all other exhibits. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit A. The declarations and attached exhibits submitted by both parties were not considered in ruling on this demurrer, as a demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or matters that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) As to the first and second causes of action, the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately pleads the elements of intentional and negligent interference with prospective business advantage and the allegations are not impermissibly inconsistent with earlier pleadings. As to the third cause of action for fraud, the SAC adequately pleads justifiable reliance with the requisite specificity. (SAC, par. 43.) rulings.Case Title: MARIJAN M. PEVEC, D.C., QME VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer as to the first, fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action sustained without leave to amend. The allegations in the First Amended Complaint fail to establish that Defendant is a "person" subject to liability under either Bus. & Prof. Code section 17200 et seq. or Labor Code section 2056. Moreover, as a government entity, Defendant can only be held liable as provided by statute (Govt. Code § 815(b)), and Plaintiff failed to provide any statutory basis for liability for the common law torts of intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage. In addition, for liability to attach to a government entity under Govt. Code section 815, the statute creating the mandatory duty must intend to protect against the kind of risk of injury suffered by the party asserting section 815.6 as a basis for liability. (California v. Sup. Ct. (Perry) (1984) 150 Cal.App. 3d 848, 854.) Here, the injury that Labor Code section 4616 is designed to forestall is an injury to employees, not to providers. Thus, there is no liability for the City for its alleged failure to comply with Labor Code section 4616. Demurrer to the second cause of action is moot pursuant to entry of dismissal of this cause of action as against Defendant. Demurrer to the third cause of action is granted with leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to allege facts specifying which conduct on behalf of Defendant constituted the deprivation of due process. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: THIEL MACRO LLC A DELAWARE LIOMITED LIABILITY VS. DAVID RYAN BRUMBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Portions Of Deft'S Oppos. To Mtn For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF THIEL MACRO LLC's Motion To Seal Portions Of Defendant's Opposition To Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Grant. No opposition filed and the motion meets the requirements of CRC 2.550 et seq. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and complies with the specific requirements of CRC 2.551(d) and (e). The prevailing party must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA WOODS VS. CBRE GROUP, INC et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT CBRE GROUP, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration On An Ind Basis And To Dismiss With Prejudice Or In The Alternative To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration.
